info.title: Worship Viewer API
info.description:
Versioned REST API under `/api/v1`. Authentication flows live at `/auth/*` (unversioned); clients should treat that split as stable for this major API generation. Public deployment metadata is available at `GET /api/v1/about` (no authentication).

**Breaking 2.0:** See `docs/api-breaking-2-0.md` for migration (`PlayerItem`, `Song.blobs` as link objects, `Session` wire model, `Problem` without `error`, PUT bodies use `Update*` types in the spec).

**Timestamps:** All timestamps are UTC and use RFC 3339 with a `Z` suffix (e.g. `2026-04-18T12:00:00Z`).

**Identifiers:** Resource IDs are opaque printable strings returned by the API; treat them as opaque and do not parse their internal structure.

**References & expand:** Cross-resource links use objects such as `BlobLink` (`{ "id": "…" }`) instead of bare id strings where noted. Session list/detail responses default to a narrow `user` link (`id` + `email`); pass `expand=user` (comma-separated with other tokens as added) to embed the full `User`. HTTP audit request counts and last-used timestamps for a single user or session are available from `GET .../metrics` routes next to each single-resource user/session endpoint, not on the `User` / `SessionBody` objects themselves.

**JSON naming:** Object keys use `snake_case`. Enum wire values use the casing shown in each schema (broader enum casing standardization is planned).

**Pagination:** List endpoints accept `page` (0-based) and `page_size` (1–500, default 50). Responses include `X-Total-Count` with the total matching rows before pagination and RFC 5988 `Link` headers (relations: first, prev, next, last) where applicable.

**Rate limiting:** Versioned `/api/v1/*` routes use token-bucket limits per client IP (`Retry-After`, `X-RateLimit-*` on **429**; configurable via server settings).

**Errors:** Failed requests return `Content-Type: application/problem+json` ([RFC 7807](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807)) with a `Problem` body (`type`, `title`, `status`, `code`, optional `detail` / `instance`). Use `detail` for human-readable text; stable machine-readable `code` values include: `unauthorized`, `forbidden`, `not_found`, `invalid_request`, `invalid_page_size`, `conflict`, `too_many_requests`, `not_acceptable`, `precondition_failed`, `internal`. Legacy schemas `ErrorResponse` and `ProblemDetails` remain listed for one release but are deprecated in favor of `Problem`.

**CSRF:** Cookie sessions use `SameSite=Lax`; state-changing methods are `POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH`/`DELETE` (not `GET`). Cross-site simple requests cannot mutate state via cookies under typical browser rules. Browser `fetch` from the SPA should use `credentials: 'same-origin'` (or include cookies only on same-site requests). API clients using bearer tokens should still avoid exposing tokens to third-party origins.

**Examples:** See schema `example` fields on core DTOs in the components section.

servers.description: Same origin as the web app (override per deployment).

get.summary: Public metadata: which build is running (no authentication).

200.description: Deployed backend metadata

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Zero-based page (default 0). Track A: `X-Total-Count` = pre-pagination total; last page when `items.len() < page_size` or empty. See `list-pagination.md`.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Items per page. Must be 1–500. Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter on stored OCR text. Whitespace-only is treated as absent.

200.description: Return all blobs. `X-Total-Count` matches the filtered total.

parameters.description: Full-text search query (titles, artists, line lyrics); uses text_search analyzer (stemming)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort: JSON:API-style comma-separated keys (`-` = descending), e.g. `-id`, `title`, `relevance` (with `q`). Legacy `id_desc` / … still accepted.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Filter: song must list this language in `data.languages`.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Filter: case-insensitive substring match on stringified `data.tags`.

201.description: Create a new song. Optional `owner` is a team id; omit for the caller's personal team. When the effective target is the personal team, default-collection behavior may apply (BLC-SONG-010). When `owner` names a different team the song is created there without that default-collection side effect. Library edit access is required on the target team.

302.description: Successful callback exchange; redirects back to frontend

401.description: OIDC user info missing required claims

parameters.name: redirect_to
parameters.description: Optional same-origin path (`/…`) to use after login. Must start with `/` and must not be `//…` or `/http…`; otherwise it is ignored and the default post-login path is used (see `sanitize_redirect`).

302.description: Redirect to OIDC provider login page

429.description: Rate limit exceeded; slow down and retry

204.description: Ends the session idempotently: clears `sso_session` cookie and deletes the session server-side if the cookie or `Authorization: Bearer` session id is present. Missing/unknown sessions still yield 204.
OTP generated and delivered out-of-band. Rate limits apply per IP (see server `auth_rate_limit_*` settings). Lockout after too many failed verify attempts is enforced on `/auth/otp/verify`.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded; slow down and retry

500.description: Failed to persist or deliver OTP

200.description: OTP verified successfully; session cookie issued. When `WORSHIP_OTP_ALLOW_SELF_SIGNUP` is unset/true, a new user may be created for an unknown email; when false, the email must already exist. Response always embeds full `User` under `user`.

400.description: OTP verification failed, or signup disabled for unknown email

429.description: Too many incorrect attempts; request a new code

git_commit.description: Git revision if `GIT_COMMIT_SHA` was set during `cargo build`.

ocr.description: OCR or extracted text used for search (may be empty).

BlobLink.description: Cross-resource reference to a blob (opaque `id` as returned by the API).

cover.description: Cover art reference (client-resolved blob id or URL).

ocr.description: OCR or extracted text used for search (may be empty).

owner.description: Owning team id (same format as `Blob.owner` in responses). Omit to create under the caller's personal team.
Owning team id (`team` record id, same format as `Collection.owner` in responses). Omit to create under the caller's personal team.
Owning team id (same format as `Setlist.owner` in responses). Omit to create under the caller's personal team.
Owning team id (same format as `Song.owner` in responses). Omit to create under the caller's personal team (and apply default-collection rules).

members.description: Additional members (besides the creating user, who is always `admin`).

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error envelope returned by all API error responses.

`code` is a stable, machine-readable identifier; `error` is a human-readable
description.

FileType.description: Stored image format for blobs. Wire values are MIME type strings.

HttpAuditLog.description: One persisted HTTP request audit row (admin monitoring API).

HttpAuditMetrics.description: HTTP request audit aggregates for a user or session (`GET .../metrics`).

MoveOwner.description: Target owning team for a library resource move (`owner` string matches GET responses).

PatchBlob.description: Partial update for a blob. Absent fields are left unchanged.

ocr.description: OCR or extracted text used for search (may be empty).

PatchCollection.description: Partial update for a collection. Absent fields are left unchanged.

PatchSetlist.description: Partial update for a setlist. Absent fields are left unchanged.

PatchSong.description: Partial update for a song. Absent fields are left unchanged.

owner.description: Set the song's owning team id; omit to leave unchanged.

PatchSongData.description:
Partial update for song metadata.

`Patch<T>` fields preserve 3-state behavior for nullable members:
- `Missing`: keep existing value
- `Null`: clear the value
- `Value(v)`: set a new value

PatchTeam.description:
Partial update for a team. Absent fields are left unchanged.

`members` uses three-state semantics:
- absent → members list is not modified
- `null` → rejected (a team must always have members)
- `[…]` → replaces the entire members list

between_items.description: In book scroll mode, whether navigation jumps between whole items.

scroll_type_cache_other_orientation.description: Scroll mode cached when toggling orientation (book/half-page behavior).

PlayerBlobItem.description: Sheet-music or image item in a player sequence (`type`: `"blob"`).

PlayerChordsItem.description: ChordPro-backed song item in a player sequence (`type`: `"chords"`).

Problem.title: Problem
Problem.description:
[RFC 7807](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807) problem document (`application/problem+json`).

Canonical error body for HTTP 4xx/5xx responses. Extension members include `code`.

instance.description: Optional URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence.

title.description: Short, stable summary of the problem class.

type.description: URI reference identifying the problem type.

ProblemDetails.title: ProblemDetails
ProblemDetails.description: Deprecated OpenAPI name for [`Problem`]; identical wire shape.

SessionBody.description:
Wire representation of a session. `user` is a [`TeamUser`] link unless the client
passes `expand=user`, in which case it is the full [`User`] object.

oneOf.description: Try full user first so link-shaped JSON (missing required user fields) falls through to [`TeamUser`].

blobs.description: Linked blob assets (`id` is the blob resource identifier).

data.description: ChordPro-derived payload (sections, lyrics, metadata); see `SongDataSchema` in the OpenAPI components.

not_a_song.description: When true, this record is not treated as a musical song (e.g. scripture or spoken content).

user_specific_addons.description: Per-request flags such as whether the current user liked this song.

SongDataSchema.description: ChordPro-derived metadata and content (titles, tags, [`sections`](https://chordpro.org/) as structured blocks).

key.description: Musical key; chord symbols use ChordPro conventions.

sections.description: Structured sections (verse, chorus, etc.) with lyric lines and chords.

tags.description: Custom meta tags from ChordPro `{meta: name value}` pairs.

titles.description: Primary and alternate titles from ChordPro `{title}` / `{title:N}` directives.

key.description: Musical key for this slot. Write bodies (e.g. PATCH) use `{ level }` (0–11, tonic pitch class from A). Responses may return `{ level }` or a legacy chord string (e.g. `G`, `Am`).

nr.description: Optional display position in the parent list (e.g. `1`, `2a`).

SongListQuery.description:
Query parameters for `GET /api/v1/songs`: pagination plus optional sort and filters.

Pagination fields mirror [`ListQuery`] (they are not `flatten`ed so `actix_web::Query`
deserializes reliably from `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` query strings).

lang.description: Filter to songs whose `data.languages` contains this string (exact match on an array element).

sort.description:
Sort: comma-separated fields, `-` prefix for descending (e.g. `-id`, `title`, `-id,title`).
Use `relevance` when searching (`q` non-empty). Legacy tokens (`id_desc`, …) accepted with a warning.

tag.description: Case-insensitive substring match against the stringified `data.tags` object (keys and values).

members.description: Everyone except the personal-team owner (if any).

oneOf.description: When set, this team is that user's personal team (1:1, not deletable). Not listed in `members`.

TeamUser.description: User slice returned on team **GET** (`id` + `email` only, same naming as `User`).

TeamUserRef.description: User reference in **POST/PUT** team payloads (`id` only).

id.description: Optional stable id when this TOC row links to a concrete song.

nr.description: Display number/label for this TOC row.

UpdateBlob.description: Full replacement body for `PUT /api/v1/blobs/{id}` metadata (same shape as [`CreateBlob`]; does not upload bytes).

ocr.description: OCR or extracted text used for search (may be empty).

owner.description: Target team id for the collection's `owner`; omit or `null` to keep the current owner.
Target team id for the setlist's `owner`; omit or `null` to keep the current owner.

UpdateSong.description: Full replacement body for `PUT /api/v1/songs/{id}` (same fields as [`CreateSong`]; server-owned `id` is path-only).

owner.description: Target team id for the song's `owner`; omit or `null` to keep the current owner.

members.description: When set, replaces the entire `members` list (shared teams must keep at least one `admin`).

avatar_blob_id.description: User-uploaded profile image; takes precedence over [`Self::oauth_avatar_blob_id`].

oauth_picture_url.description: Last `picture` claim URL seen from OIDC (used to detect when to re-fetch the cached avatar).

level.description: Pitch-class index for the slot key tonic (0=A, 1=Bb, … 11=Ab); matches `Song.data.key` structured form.

SessionCookie.name: sso_session
SessionCookie.description: Session cookie returned after a successful authentication flow

tags.name: About
tags.description: Public server build and environment metadata (`GET /api/v1/about`).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: OAuth/OIDC login, OTP email codes, and logout. Session cookies are set on successful auth (see authentication BLC).

tags.name: Monitoring
tags.description: Admin-only operational metrics and request audit listings under `/monitoring/`.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Current user (`/users/me`), directory listing, sessions (own and admin), and admin user lifecycle.

tags.name: Songs
tags.description: Song CRUD, player JSON, likes, search/sort listing.

tags.name: Collections
tags.description: Owned song collections, nested songs, and player views.

tags.name: Blobs
tags.description: Binary image assets: metadata, byte upload/download with cache headers.

tags.name: Setlists
tags.description: Ordered sets of songs and player payloads for services.

tags.name: Teams
tags.description: Team membership, roles, and invitations (nested under `/teams/{id}/invitations`).
